{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "200:     output [NumHarts-1:0][1:0]          irq_o         // level sensitive IR lines, mip & sip (async)
201: );
202: 
203:   localparam int unsigned AxiIdWidth    =  1;
204:   localparam int unsigned AxiAddrWidth  = 64;
205:   localparam int unsigned AxiDataWidth  = 64;
206:   localparam int unsigned AxiUserWidth  =  1;
207: 
208: 
209: 
210:   parameter  FUSE_MEM_SIZE = 150; // change this size when ever no of entries in FUSE mem is changed
211:   parameter NB_SLAVE = 1;
212:   parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 14;
213: 
214:   logic [8*NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] reglk_ctrl; 
215:   logic [4*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   acc_ctrl; // Access control values
216:   logic [3:0][NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] acc_ctrl_c; 
217: 
218:    genvar i, j;
219:    generate
220:        for (i=0; i < 4; i++) begin
221:         for (j=0; j < NB_PERIPHERALS; j++) begin
222:            assign acc_ctrl_c[i][j] = acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]);
223:         end
224:        end 
225:    endgenerate 
226: 
227: 
228: 
229:   /////////////////////////////
230:   // Debug module and JTAG
231:   /////////////////////////////
232: 
233:   logic          debug_req_valid;
234:   logic          debug_req_ready;
235:   logic          debug_resp_valid;
236:   logic          debug_resp_ready;
237: 
238:   logic [255:0]  jtag_hash, ikey_hash, okey_hash;
239:   logic           jtag_unlock; 
240: 
241:   dm::dmi_req_t  debug_req;
242:   dm::dmi_resp_t debug_resp;
243: 
244: `ifdef RISCV_FESVR_SIM
245: 
246:   initial begin
247:     $display("[INFO] instantiating FESVR DTM in simulation.");
248:   end
249: 
250:   // SiFive's SimDTM Module
251:   // Converts to DPI calls
252:   logic [31:0] sim_exit; // TODO: wire this up in the testbench
253:   logic [1:0] debug_req_bits_op;
254:   assign dmi_req.op = dm::dtm_op_t'(debug_req_bits_op);
255: 
256:   SimDTM i_SimDTM (
257:     .clk                  ( clk_i                ),
258:     .reset                ( ~rst_ni              ),
259:     .debug_req_valid      ( debug_req_valid      ),
260:     .debug_req_ready      ( debug_req_ready      ),
261:     .debug_req_bits_addr  ( debug_req.addr       ),
262:     .debug_req_bits_op    ( debug_req_bits_op    ),
263:     .debug_req_bits_data  ( debug_req.data       ),
264:     .debug_resp_valid     ( debug_resp_valid     ),
265:     .debug_resp_ready     ( debug_resp_ready     ),
266:     .debug_resp_bits_resp ( debug_resp.resp      ),
267:     .debug_resp_bits_data ( debug_resp.data      ),
268:     .exit                 ( sim_exit             )
269:   );
270: 
271: `else // RISCV_FESVR_SIM
272: 
273:   logic        tck, tms, trst_n, tdi, tdo, tdo_oe;
274: 
275:   dmi_jtag i_dmi_jtag (
276:     .clk_i                                ,
277:     .rst_ni                               ,
278:     .testmode_i                           ,
279:     .we_flag          ( we_flag_4        ),
280:     .jtag_hash_i      ( jtag_hash        ),
281:     .okey_hash_i      ( okey_hash        ),
282:     .ikey_hash_i      ( ikey_hash        ),
283:     .jtag_unlock_o    ( jtag_unlock      ),
284:     .dmi_req_o        ( debug_req        ),
285:     .dmi_req_valid_o  ( debug_req_valid  ),
286:     .dmi_req_ready_i  ( debug_req_ready  ),
287:     .dmi_resp_i       ( debug_resp       ),
288:     .dmi_resp_ready_o ( debug_resp_ready ),
289:     .dmi_resp_valid_i ( debug_resp_valid ),
290:     .dmi_rst_no       (                  ), // not connected
291:     .tck_i            ( tck              ),
292:     .tms_i            ( tms              ),
293:     .trst_ni          ( trst_n           ),
294:     .td_i             ( tdi              ),
295:     .td_o             ( tdo              ),
296:     .tdo_oe_o         ( tdo_oe           )
297:   );
298: 
299: `ifdef RISCV_JTAG_SIM
300: 
301:   initial begin
302:     $display("[INFO] instantiating JTAG DTM in simul

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "500:     .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AxiIdWidth    ),
501:     .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AxiAddrWidth  ),
502:     .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AxiDataWidth  ),
503:     .AXI_USER_WIDTH ( AxiUserWidth  )
504:   ) i_axi2rom (
505:     .clk_i                ,
506:     .rst_ni               ,
507:     .slave  ( br_master  ),
508:     .req_o  ( rom_req_acct  ),
509:     .we_o   (            ),
510:     .addr_o ( rom_addr   ),
511:     .be_o   (            ),
512:     .data_o (            ),
513:     .data_i ( rom_rdata  )
514:   );
515: 
516:   //assign rom_req = rom_req_acct ; //&& acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]; 
517:   assign rom_req = rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]; 
518: 
519:   bootrom i_bootrom_bm (
520:     .clk_i                   ,
521:     .req_i      ( rom_req   ),
522:     .addr_i     ( rom_addr  ),
523:     .rdata_o    ( rom_rdata_bm )
524:   );
525: 
526:   bootrom_linux i_bootrom_linux (
527:     .clk_i                   ,
528:     .req_i      ( rom_req   ),
529:     .addr_i     ( rom_addr  ),
530:     .rdata_o    ( rom_rdata_linux )
531:   );
532: 
533:   // we want to run in baremetal mode when using pitonstream
534:   assign rom_rdata = (ariane_boot_sel_i) ? rom_rdata_linux : rom_rdata_linux;
535: 
536:   noc_axilite_bridge #(
537:     .SLAVE_RESP_BYTEWIDTH   ( 8             ),
538:     .SWAP_ENDIANESS         ( SwapEndianess )
539:   ) i_bootrom_axilite_bridge (
540:     .clk                    ( clk_i                           ),
541:     .rst                    ( ~rst_ni                         ),
542:     // to/from NOC
543:     .splitter_bridge_val    ( buf_ariane_bootrom_noc2_valid_i ),
544:     .splitter_bridge_data   ( buf_ariane_bootrom_noc2_data_i  ),
545:     .bridge_splitter_rdy    ( ariane_bootrom_buf_noc2_ready_o ),
546:     .bridge_splitter_val    ( ariane_bootrom_buf_noc3_valid_o ),
547:     .bridge_splitter_data   ( ariane_bootrom_buf_noc3_data_o  ),
548:     .splitter_bridge_rdy    ( buf_ariane_bootrom_noc3_ready_i ),
549:     //axi lite signals
550:     //write address channel
551:     .m_axi_awaddr           ( br_master.aw_addr               ),
552:     .m_axi_awvalid          ( br_master.aw_valid              ),
553:     .m_axi_awready          ( br_master.aw_ready              ),
554:     //write data channel
555:     .m_axi_wdata            ( br_master.w_data                ),
556:     .m_axi_wstrb            ( br_master.w_strb                ),
557:     .m_axi_wvalid           ( br_master.w_valid               ),
558:     .m_axi_wready           ( br_master.w_ready               ),
559:     //read address channel
560:     .m_axi_araddr           ( br_master.ar_addr               ),
561:     .m_axi_arvalid          ( br_master.ar_valid              ),
562:     .m_axi_arready          ( br_master.ar_ready              ),
563:     //read data channel
564:     .m_axi_rdata            ( br_master.r_data                ),
565:     .m_axi_rresp            ( br_master.r_resp                ),
566:     .m_axi_rvalid           ( br_master.r_valid               ),
567:     .m_axi_rready           ( br_master.r_ready               ),
568:     //write response channel
569:     .m_axi_bresp            ( br_master.b_resp                ),
570:     .m_axi_bvalid           ( br_master.b_valid               ),
571:     .m_axi_bready           ( br_master.b_ready               ),
572:     // non-axi-lite signals
573:     .w_reqbuf_size          (                                 ),
574:     .r_reqbuf_size          (                                 )
575:   );
576: 
577:   // tie off signals not used by AXI-lite
578:   assign br_master.aw_id     = '0;
579:   assign br_master.aw_len    = '0;
580:   assign br_master.aw_size   = 3'b11;// 8byte
581:   assign br_master.aw_burst  = '0;
582:   assign br_master.aw_lock   = '0;
583:   assign br_master.aw_cache  = '0;
584:   assign br_master.aw_prot   = '0;

8: // CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
9: // specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
10: //
11: // Author: Florian Zaruba, ETH Zurich
12: // Date: 17/07/2017
13: // Description: AXI Lite compatible interface
14: //
15: 
16: module axi_lite_interface #(
17:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
18:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
19:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10
20: ) (
21:     input logic                       clk_i,    // Clock
22:     input logic                       rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
23: 
24:     input  ariane_axi::req_t          axi_req_i,
25:     output ariane_axi::resp_t         axi_resp_o,
26: 
27:     output logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_o,
28:     output logic                      en_o,        // transaction is valid
29:     output logic                      we_o,        // write
30:     input  logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_i,      // data
31:     output logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_o
32: );
33: 
34:     // The RLAST signal is not required, and is considered asserted for every transfer on the read data channel.
35:     enum logic [1:0] { IDLE, READ, WRITE, WRITE_B } state_q, state_d;
36:     // save the trans id, we will need it for reflection otherwise we are not plug compatible to the AXI standard
37:     logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0]   trans_id_n, trans_id_q;
38:     // address register
39:     logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_n,  address_q;
40: 
41:     // pass through read data on the read data channel
42:     assign axi_resp_o.r.data = data_i;
43:     // send back the transaction id we've latched
44:     assign axi_resp_o.r.id = trans_id_q;
45:     assign axi_resp_o.b.id = trans_id_q;
46:     // set r_last to one as defined by the AXI4 - Lite standard
47:     assign axi_resp_o.r.last = 1'b1;
48:     // we do not support any errors so set response flag to all zeros
49:     assign axi_resp_o.b.resp = 2'b0;
50:     assign axi_resp_o.r.resp = 2'b0;
51:     // output data which we want to write to the slave
52:     assign data_o = axi_req_i.w.data;
53:     // ------------------------
54:     // AXI4-Lite State Machine
55:     // ------------------------
56:     always_comb begin
57:         // default signal assignment
58:         state_d    = state_q;
59:         address_n  = address_q;
60:         trans_id_n = trans_id_q;
61: 
62:         // we'll answer a write request only if we got address and data
63:         axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b0;
64:         axi_resp_o.w_ready  = 1'b0;
65:         axi_resp_o.b_valid  = 1'b0;
66: 
67:         axi_resp_o.ar_ready = 1'b0;
68:         axi_resp_o.r_valid  = 1'b0;
69: 
70:         address_o      = '0;
71:         we_o           = 1'b0;
72:         en_o           = 1'b0;
73: 
74:         case (state_q)
75:             // we are ready to accept a new request
76:             IDLE: begin
77:                 // we've git a valid write request, we also know that we have asserted the aw_ready
78:                 if (axi_req_i.aw_valid) begin
79:                     axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b1;
80:                     // this costs performance but the interconnect does not obey the AXI standard
81:                     // e.g.: we could wait for aw_valid && w_valid to do the transaction.
82:                     state_d = WRITE;
83:                     // save address
84:                     address_n = axi_req_i.aw
[TRUNCATED]

1: // Copyright 2019 ETH Zurich and University of Bologna.
2: // Copyright and related rights are licensed under the Solderpad Hardware
3: // License, Version 0.51 (the "License"); you may not use this file except in
4: // compliance with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
5: // http://solderpad.org/licenses/SHL-0.51. Unless required by applicable law
6: // or agreed to in writing, software, hardware and materials distributed under
7: // this License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
8: // CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
9: // specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
10: //
11: // Author: Moritz Schneider, ETH Zurich
12: // Date: 2.10.2019
13: // Description: purely combinatorial PMP unit (with extraction for more complex configs such as NAPOT)
14: 
15: module pmp #(
16:     parameter int unsigned XLEN = 32,       // rv64: 64
17:     parameter int unsigned PMP_LEN = 32,    // rv64: 54
18:     parameter int unsigned NR_ENTRIES = 4
19: ) (
20:     // Input
21:     input logic [XLEN-1:0] addr_i,
22:     input riscv::pmp_access_t access_type_i,
23:     input riscv::priv_lvl_t priv_lvl_i,
24:     // Configuration
25:     input logic [NR_ENTRIES-1:0][PMP_LEN-1:0] conf_addr_i,
26:     input riscv::pmpcfg_t [NR_ENTRIES-1:0] conf_i,
27:     // Output
28:     output logic allow_o
29: );
30:     // if there are no PMPs we can always grant the access.
31:     if (NR_ENTRIES > 0) begin : gen_pmp
32:         logic [NR_ENTRIES-1:0] match;
33: 
34:         for (genvar i = 0; i < NR_ENTRIES; i++) begin
35:             pmp_entry #(
36:                 .XLEN    ( XLEN    ),
37:                 .PMP_LEN ( PMP_LEN )
38:             ) i_pmp_entry(
39:                 .addr_i           ( addr_i                         ),
40:                 .conf_addr_i      ( conf_addr_i[i]                 ),
41:                 .conf_addr_prev_i ( i == 0 ? '0 : conf_addr_i[i-1] ),
42:                 .conf_addr_mode_i ( conf_i[i].addr_mode            ),
43:                 .match_o          ( match[i]                       )
44:             );
45:         end
46: 
47:         always_comb begin
48:             int i;
49:             
50:             allow_o = 1'b0;
51:             for (i = 0; i < NR_ENTRIES; i++) begin
52:                 // either we are in S or U mode or the config is locked in which
53:                 // case it also applies in M mode
54:                 if (priv_lvl_i != riscv::PRIV_LVL_M || conf_i[i].locked) begin
55:                     if (match[i]) begin
56:                         if ((access_type_i & conf_i[i].access_type) != access_type_i) allow_o = 1'b0;
57:                         else allow_o = 1'b1;
58:                         break;
59:                     end
60:                 end
61:             end
62:             if (i == NR_ENTRIES) begin // no PMP entry matched the address
63:                 // allow all accesses from M-mode for no pmp match
64:                 if (priv_lvl_i == riscv::PRIV_LVL_M) allow_o = 1'b1;
65:                 // disallow accesses for all other modes
66:                 else allow_o = 1'b0;
67:             end
68:         end
69:     end else assign allow_o = 1'b1;
70: 
71:     `ifdef FORMAL
72:     always @(*) begin
73:         if(priv_lvl_i == riscv::PRIV_LVL_M) begin
74:             static logic no_locked = 1'b1;
75:             for (int i = 0; i < NR_ENTRIES; i++) begin
76:                 if (conf_i[i].locked && conf_i[i].addr_mode != riscv::OFF) begin
77:                     no_locked &= 1'b0;
78:                 end else no_locked &= 1'b1;
79:             end
80:             
81:             if (no_locked == 1'b1) assert(allow_o == 1'b1);
82:         end
83:     end
84:     `endif
85: endmodule

1: // Copyright 2019 ETH Zurich and University of Bologna.
2: // Copyright and related rights are licensed under the Solderpad Hardware
3: // License, Version 0.51 (the "License"); you may not use this file except in
4: // compliance with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
5: // http://solderpad.org/licenses/SHL-0.51. Unless required by applicable law
6: // or agreed to in writing, software, hardware and materials distributed under
7: // this License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
8: // CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
9: // specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
10: //
11: // Author: Moritz Schneider, ETH Zurich
12: // Date: 2.10.2019
13: // Description: single PMP entry
14: 
15: module pmp_entry #(
16:     parameter int unsigned XLEN = 64,
17:     parameter int unsigned PMP_LEN = 54
18: ) (
19:     // Input
20:     input logic [XLEN-1:0] addr_i,
21: 
22:     // Configuration
23:     input logic [PMP_LEN-1:0] conf_addr_i,
24:     input logic [PMP_LEN-1:0] conf_addr_prev_i,
25:     input riscv::pmp_addr_mode_t conf_addr_mode_i,
26: 
27:     // Output
28:     output logic match_o
29: );
30:     logic [XLEN-1:0] conf_addr_n;
31:     logic [$clog2(XLEN)-1:0] trail_ones;
32:     assign conf_addr_n = ~conf_addr_i;
33:     lzc #(.WIDTH(XLEN), .MODE(1'b0)) i_lzc(
34:         .in_i    ( conf_addr_n ),
35:         .cnt_o   ( trail_ones  ),
36:         .empty_o (             )
37:     );
38: 
39:     always_comb begin
40:         case (conf_addr_mode_i)
41:             riscv::TOR:     begin
42:                 // check that the requested address is in between the two 
43:                 // configuration addresses
44:                 if (addr_i >= (conf_addr_prev_i << 2) && addr_i < (conf_addr_i << 2)) begin
45:                     match_o = 1'b1;
46:                 end else match_o = 1'b0;
47: 
48:                 `ifdef FORMAL
49:                 if (match_o == 0) begin
50:                     assert(addr_i >= (conf_addr_i << 2) || addr_i < (conf_addr_prev_i << 2));
51:                 end else begin
52:                     assert(addr_i < (conf_addr_i << 2) || addr_i >= (conf_addr_prev_i << 2));
53:                 end
54:                 `endif
55:             end
56:             riscv::NA4, riscv::NAPOT:   begin
57:                 logic [XLEN-1:0] base;
58:                 logic [XLEN-1:0] mask;
59:                 int unsigned size;
60: 
61:                 if (conf_addr_mode_i == riscv::NA4) size = 2;
62:                 else begin
63:                     // use the extracted trailing ones
64:                     size = trail_ones+3;
65:                 end
66: 
67:                 mask = '1 << size;
68:                 base = (conf_addr_i << 2) & mask;
69:                 match_o = (addr_i & mask) == base ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
70: 
71:                 `ifdef FORMAL
72:                 // size extract checks
73:                 assert(size >= 2);
74:                 if (conf_addr_mode_i == riscv::NAPOT) begin
75:                     assert(size > 2);
76:                     if (size < PMP_LEN) assert(conf_addr_i[size - 3] == 0);
77:                     for (int i = 0; i < PMP_LEN; i++) begin
78:                         if (size > 3 && i <= size - 4) begin
79:                             assert(conf_addr_i[i] == 1); // check that all the rest are ones
80:                         end
81:                     end
82:                 end
83: 
84:                 if (size < XLEN-1) begin
85:                     if (base + 2**size > base) begin // check for overflow
86:                         if (match_o == 0) begin
87:                             assert(addr_i >= base + 2**size || addr_i < base);
88:                         end else begin
89:                             assert(addr_i < base + 2**size && addr_i >= base);
90:                         end
91:                     end else begin
92:                         
[TRUNCATED]

949:     .rst                    ( ~rst_ni                       ),
950:     // to/from NOC
951:     .splitter_bridge_val    ( buf_ariane_rst_noc2_valid_i ),
952:     .splitter_bridge_data   ( buf_ariane_rst_noc2_data_i  ),
953:     .bridge_splitter_rdy    ( ariane_rst_buf_noc2_ready_o ),
954:     .bridge_splitter_val    ( ariane_rst_buf_noc3_valid_o ),
955:     .bridge_splitter_data   ( ariane_rst_buf_noc3_data_o  ),
956:     .splitter_bridge_rdy    ( buf_ariane_rst_noc3_ready_i ),
957:     //axi lite signals
958:     //write address channel
959:     .m_axi_awaddr           ( rst_axi_req.aw.addr         ),
960:     .m_axi_awvalid          ( rst_axi_req.aw_valid        ),
961:     .m_axi_awready          ( rst_axi_resp.aw_ready       ),
962:     //write data channel

966:     .m_axi_wready           ( rst_axi_resp.w_ready        ),
967:     //read address channel
968:     .m_axi_araddr           ( rst_axi_req.ar.addr         ),

970:     .m_axi_arready          ( rst_axi_resp.ar_ready       ),
971:     //read data channel
972:     .m_axi_rdata            ( rst_axi_resp.r.data         ),
973:     .m_axi_rresp            ( rst_axi_resp.r.resp         ),
974:     .m_axi_rvalid           ( rst_axi_resp.r_valid        ),

      "expected_guard": "DMI/debug requests should be mediated by jtag_unlock or equivalent debug authorization before reaching dm_top control paths.",
      "observed_behavior": "The top-level connects dmi_jtag/SimDTM DMI request/response signals directly to dm_top. jtag_unlock is produced by dmi_jtag but is not connected to dm_top or used to gate debug_req_valid at this level; it is only visibly passed later to reglk_wrapper. In RISCV_FESVR_SIM, SimDTM drives debug_req_valid/debug_req without visible unlock mediation.",
      "path": "tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i or SimDTM -> dmi_jtag/SimDTM -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top -> ndmreset_o/dmactive_o/debug_req_o",
      "state_condition": "jtag_unlock reset/default and dmi_jtag internal policy are not visible here; top-level does not gate dm_top DMI inputs with jtag_unlock. SimDTM path is compile-time gated by RISCV_FESVR_SIM but, if included, bypasses jtag_unlock entirely.",
      "impact": "An unauthenticated or insufficiently authorized debug requester may be able to activate the debug module, request hart debug entry, or assert non-debug-module reset through DMI if dmi_jtag/SimDTM does not fully enforce authorization internally.",
      "rtl_evidence": [
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:238-239 declares jtag_hash/ikey_hash/okey_hash and jtag_unlock.",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:256-269 SimDTM drives debug_req_valid, debug_req fields, and receives debug_resp without visible auth guard.",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:275-289 dmi_jtag connects jtag hashes and outputs jtag_unlock, but also directly outputs debug_req/debug_req_valid.",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:353-388 dm_top consumes debug_req_valid/debug_req and drives ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, debug_req_o.",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:1814-1821 shows jtag_unlock visibly connected to reglk_wrapper, not to dm_top."
